posts.name: Glen Gold
posts.cooked: I feel your pain. When I was trying to track that down a couple years ago, it was pretty baffling. It’s referred to on some websites as “1923” but that doesn’t appear on the bottle anywhere, and putting “1923” into wine-searcher just confuses everything. I notice that on wine-searcher pro, there’s at least one place that has it, though.

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked:
I am being told that the VV is for POB ( Pearl of Burgundy in Hong Kong ). What does that mean?

Update: pearl of burgundy is their exclusive importer in Hong Kong.

Probably means the wine is not in the US

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: I just texted Pierre Duroché to get you guys a definitive answer. He replied that the bulk of the cuvée is indeed taken by Pearl of Burgundy, who do sell some in the USA in the NYC and Chicago markets. Some is also sold to his importers in the UK, Norway, and Lebanon.
